title: Heat vs. Temperature: Understanding Thermal Energy
summary: This video explores heat and temperature – two fundamental concepts in physics that explain how objects interact energetically.

sections.heading: What is Heat?
sections.body: Heat refers to the transfer of thermal energy between bodies due to a temperature difference. It's an essential concept that helps us understand how objects exchange warmth.

sections.heading: What is Temperature?
sections.body: Temperature measures the average kinetic energy of particles within a substance, representing how 'hot' or 'cold' an object feels. It plays a crucial role in determining heat exchange.

sections.heading: Heat Transfer and Temperature Change
sections.body: When two objects with different temperatures come into contact, heat flows from the warmer to cooler object until they reach equilibrium. This process can cause temperature changes that we experience in daily life.

key_facts: Heat transfer occurs via conduction, convection, and radiation.
